              generic "Complete: The On-Board Values Obfuscation technique (DE-0003) fits within the Defense Evasion tactic (ST0006) because it involves targeting onboard values that prevent malicious commands from being processed. By obfuscating these values, threat actors bypass the vehicle's security controls, thereby avoiding detection of their malicious activities." [e126] [cursor=pointer]

              generic "Return exactly one top-level JSON object with keys: - pairs - skipped_reason Generate up to 2 grounded, non-duplicative QRA pairs about the relationship between this SPARTA technique and its parent tactic. ## Technique Technique ID: DE-0003 Technique Name: On-Board Values Obfuscation Technique Description: Threat actors may target various onboard values put in place to prevent malicious or poorly crafted commands from being processed. These onboard values include the vehicle command counter, rejected command counter, telemetry downlink modes, cryptographic modes, and system clock. ## Parent Tactic Parent Tactic ID: ST0006 Parent Tactic Name: Defense Evasion Parent Tactic Description: Threat actor is trying to avoid being detected. ## Reminders - This prompt is about the relationship, not standalone node definitions. - Use evidence from both the technique and the tactic. - Preserve modality exactly. - Prefer fewer pairs over weak pairs. - If source support is insufficient, return zero pairs." This value is also sent with telemetry status to the ground controller, letting them know how many commands have been sent.
